title: Bootstrap x0x-symphony repo with architecture and ADRs
description: Create the sibling repo skeleton (Cargo.toml, README, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, justfile, LICENSE), write the M0 architecture document at docs/design/symphony.md, and commit four ADRs that lock the structural decisions: tracker abstraction, sharded ownership with TTL, no external tracker in v1.0, and x0x TaskList as the symphony backbone.
acceptance: Repo at ../x0x-symphony exists with skeleton files
docs/design/symphony.md is committed and authoritative
docs/adr/0001..0004 are all marked Accepted
issues/{README.md, schema.md, issues.jsonl} are committed
WORKFLOW.md exists for symphony's own dogfooding
validation: Cross-check ADR statuses against the README index

handoff.summary: M0 architecture frozen: sibling repo bootstrapped, four ADRs accepted, design doc and convergence note committed, symphony-side bootstrap tracker seeded.

validation.command: python3 -c "import json,pathlib\nfor l in pathlib.Path('issues/issues.jsonl').read_text().splitlines():\n  if l.strip(): json.loads(l)"

follow_up: Decide on a GitHub remote and push (out of scope for M0)
Sync the Obsidian vault note for the new project

title: Define Tracker / Runner / Workspace traits as a runnable crate
description: Add the first crate `crates/x0x-symphony-core` to the workspace. Implement the trait surfaces from ADR-0001: Tracker, Runner, Workspace, plus the supporting types (Issue, Claim, Handoff, ReleaseReason, RunnerCapabilities, SessionContext, SessionHandle, TurnOutcome, UsageReport, EventStream, PollContext). Traits only — no adapter implementations yet. Ship full rustdoc on every public item. Every test must pass and clippy must be clean with -D warnings.
acceptance: crates/x0x-symphony-core/ exists in the workspace
Tracker, Runner, Workspace traits compile with documented public surface
No adapter implementations are landed in this issue
Crate has at least one trait round-trip test using a stub impl
just fmt-check && just lint && just test all pass

title: Implement git_jsonl Tracker adapter
description: Add `crates/x0x-symphony-tracker-git-jsonl` implementing the Tracker trait against `issues/issues.jsonl`. Use git index lock for serialization on a single host; commit each state transition. Includes deterministic ID allocation, blocker checks, and JSONL schema validation on read. Throwaway adapter — explicit module-level doc comment notes M1–M2 lifespan and points at ADR-0003.
acceptance: Adapter implements Tracker against issues/issues.jsonl
Round-trip integration test creates, claims, heartbeats, and hands off an issue
Schema violations are reported with structured errors, never panics
Module-level doc records lifespan and supersession path

title: Implement shell Runner with codex and claude_code presets
description: Add `crates/x0x-symphony-runner-shell` implementing the Runner trait by spawning a child process with prompt on stdin, streaming stdout/stderr, capturing exit + duration + minimal usage. Add two thin preset configurations (codex, claude_code) that set command + args + env without bespoke logic. Per ADR-0001, the shell runner is the canonical contract; bespoke runner crates are deferred until a harness's structured event stream is worth parsing.
acceptance: Adapter implements Runner against arbitrary child processes
codex and claude_code preset configs are documented and tested
Output streaming does not buffer stdout/stderr indefinitely
Hook timeout is enforced via tokio::time::timeout, not signals

title: Workspace manager with hook execution and root containment
description: Add `crates/x0x-symphony-workspace` with deterministic per-issue paths under a configured root, hook execution (after_create, before_run, after_run, before_remove) with timeouts, and root containment enforcement (sanitized issue IDs, refusal to operate on paths that escape the root). Workspaces are preserved across retries and cleaned on terminal states.
acceptance: Workspace paths are deterministic from sanitized issue IDs
Symbolic links and traversal sequences are rejected at creation
Hook timeouts are enforced and produce structured outcomes
Cleanup respects retry semantics — only terminal states delete

title: Orchestrator: poll loop, dispatch, concurrency, retry
description: Add `crates/x0x-symphony-orchestrator` wiring the Tracker + Runner + Workspace + claim manager. Single-node operation. Polling loop driven by polling.interval_ms. Concurrency limits per agent.max_concurrent_agents and per-state caps. Exponential backoff capped at agent.max_retry_backoff_ms. On startup, reconcile any in-progress claim owned by this agent (resume if heartbeat fresh, release if stale).
acceptance: End-to-end smoke test: pick a todo issue, run a stub shell runner, write review back
Concurrency caps are tested with a forced contention scenario
Reconciliation logic is tested with mocked time
No unwrap/expect/panic in production paths

title: Daemon and CLI binaries (x0x-symphonyd, x0x-symphony)
description: Add `crates/x0x-symphony-bin` exposing the daemon (`x0x-symphonyd`) and CLI (`x0x-symphony`). Daemon runs the orchestrator. CLI subcommands at minimum: tasks (list), claim, handoff, status, proofs (list/show), config (show/check). Both binaries read WORKFLOW.md for configuration; CLI talks to daemon over local HTTP.
acceptance: x0x-symphonyd starts, loads WORKFLOW.md, runs the poll loop
x0x-symphony tasks lists active issues
x0x-symphony status shows active claims and last events
Help text is generated and reviewed for every subcommand

title: M1 operator and runner-authoring guides
description: Write docs/symphony/operator.md (configuring WORKFLOW.md, starting the daemon, common operations) and docs/symphony/runner-authoring.md (the Runner trait, the shell runner contract, how to add a preset). Both must reference the design doc and ADRs rather than restating them. Update docs/symphony/README.md as an index.
acceptance: docs/symphony/operator.md exists and is consistent with shipped behaviour
docs/symphony/runner-authoring.md exists with a worked example
docs/symphony/README.md indexes both
Obsidian vault is synced
validation: Manual review against the running daemon
